<!-- Peblla Marketing Team Knowledge Base — Auto-generated from group chat learnings -->

# Video Script Outline Standard

## Template Reference

All video script outlines and creative proposals must follow this structure and level of detail:

**Template Document**: https://riverroad.feishu.cn/docx/B5OQdf4LhoukrAxXq6FcSDY5nNc
(SMS Marketing V2 Creative Proposal)

## Required Sections

### 1. Project Overview

- **Target**: What the video aims to achieve
- **Audience**: Who the video is for
- **Value Proposition**: Core message
- **Specifications**: Duration, format, resolution

### 2. Creative Concept

- **Storyline**: Narrative arc
- **Emotional Arc**: Emotional beats across the video
- **Visual Style**: Color palette, lighting direction, camera language
- **Motif**: Recurring visual or thematic element

### 3. Complete Shot-by-Shot Script

Every shot must include:

| Element | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Visual** | What appears on screen |
| **Camera** | Camera angle and position |
| **Movement** | Camera movement (pan, tilt, dolly, static) |
| **Lighting** | Lighting setup and mood |
| **Color Tone** | Color grading direction |
| **Sound** | Sound effects |
| **Music** | BGM direction |
| **Narrative Function** | Role of this shot in the story |
| **Feature Showcase** | Which product feature is demonstrated |
| **UI Display** | Specific UI elements shown |
| **Text Overlay** | On-screen text/titles |
| **Transition** | How this shot connects to the next |

### 4. Seedance 2.0 Prompts

- **Must be written in Chinese** (Seedance/Jimeng is a Chinese app)
- Each prompt must include the overall visual style reference (not just in the doc header)

### 5. Production Checklist

| Phase | Contents |
|-------|----------|
| **Pre-production** | Asset list, set prep, casting |
| **Production** | Shot schedule, equipment |
| **Post-production** | Editing notes, VFX, color grading |
| **Asset Requirements** | UI screenshots, product images, stock footage |

Each item must specify: **responsible role + tool used**.

### 6. Publishing Recommendations

- Metadata (title, description, tags) — handled by Nana
- Thumbnail strategy
- Publish timing
- Companion content (blog post, social clips)

### 7. Risks & Considerations

Potential issues and mitigation strategies.

### 8. Review Flow

Which reviewers, in what order, with what criteria.

### 9. Appendix

Reference materials, mood boards, competitor examples.

## Mandatory Rules

### Transitions (Permanent — 刘紫涵)

Every shot-to-shot transition must be **explicitly described**:
- Hard cut, dissolve, swipe, animated transition, etc.
- Whether text or graphics accompany the transition

### Task Assignment

Every item in asset lists, prep lists, and publishing suggestions must specify:
- **Which role** is responsible
- **What tool** they use

### Quick-Cut Style

For ~50-second videos, use quick-cut format (reference Square video style):
- Sufficient shot count — not just a few long takes
- Fast-paced, engaging rhythm

### Story Coherence

Shots must have **narrative logic** connecting them. The video should not feel like a feature-list slideshow.

### Ad-Film Structure (Permanent — 刘紫涵)

Product introduction videos should use simple, direct quick-cut ad structure:

```
Pain points (quick cuts)
  → Product reveal
    → Feature highlights (text effects + visuals)
      → CTA
```

Intersperse high-quality product UI quick cuts throughout. Avoid complex narratives (multiple protagonists, parallel storylines, etc.).

### Title Handling

- **Rex does not handle titles** — story titles go to Nana for SEO optimization
